title: Respiratory Therapy Assessment and Plan
author: James Parker, RRT

sections.reasonForConsult: O2 therapy initiation and incentive spirometry for community-acquired pneumonia with hypoxia
sections.patientBackground: 69-year-old male admitted with community-acquired pneumonia, RLL consolidation on CXR. Significant comorbidities include HFrEF (EF 30-35%), CKD 3b, and 30 pack-year smoking history (quit 15 years ago). Presenting SpO2 89% on room air.

respiratoryAssessment.breathSounds: Decreased breath sounds RLL with coarse crackles. Scattered rhonchi bilateral lower lobes. No wheezing. Left lung with mild bibasilar fine crackles (attributed to chronic HF changes).
respiratoryAssessment.respiratoryPattern: Mildly labored at rest, rate 24/min. Using accessory muscles minimally. Speaking in full sentences with occasional pauses.
respiratoryAssessment.cough: Productive — yellowish-green sputum, moderate volume. Cough effort adequate. Sputum specimen collected and sent to lab.
respiratoryAssessment.chestWallMovement: Symmetric. Reduced expansion at right base.
respiratoryAssessment.spo2Trend: 89% on RA → 94% on 2L NC within 5 minutes of initiation

oxygenTherapy.rationale: Conservative O2 target (not >96%) due to HFrEF — excessive supplemental O2 can increase afterload and is not beneficial in cardiac patients. Avoiding high-flow O2 unless clinically indicated.
oxygenTherapy.responseToTherapy: SpO2 improved from 89% to 94% on 2L NC. Patient reports subjective improvement in dyspnea. Respiratory rate decreased from 24 to 20/min within 15 minutes of O2 initiation.
oxygenTherapy.escalationPlan: If SpO2 <92% on 2L NC or RR >30: increase to 4L NC. If SpO2 <90% on 4L NC: switch to simple face mask 6-8 L/min and notify physician. If FiO2 requirements exceed 40% or patient shows signs of respiratory distress: evaluate for high-flow nasal cannula or non-invasive ventilation, notify physician immediately.

incentiveSpirometry.reducedDue: Acute pneumonia, underlying HFrEF with reduced functional capacity, deconditioning, 30 pack-year smoking history
incentiveSpirometry.technique: Demonstrated proper use: slow deep inspiration, hold at peak for 3-5 seconds, exhale slowly. Patient performed return demonstration — adequate technique. Coached on splinting right side if cough is painful.
incentiveSpirometry.documentation: IS log placed at bedside. Nursing and RT to document volumes each session.

nebulizer.ordered: Albuterol 2.5 mg via nebulizer q4h PRN for wheezing or bronchospasm
nebulizer.currentStatus: Not administered at this time — no active wheezing on assessment
nebulizer.note: Patient has no history of asthma or COPD. Bronchospasm unlikely but nebulizer available PRN given pneumonia and smoking history. Monitor for development of reactive airway symptoms.

chestPhysiotherapy.technique: Percussion and postural drainage targeting right lower lobe
chestPhysiotherapy.precautions: Avoid Trendelenburg positioning given HFrEF (risk of worsening dyspnea). Use modified postural drainage — right side lying with head of bed at 30 degrees. Monitor SpO2 during therapy. Discontinue if SpO2 <90% or patient becomes significantly dyspneic.
chestPhysiotherapy.firstSession: Performed today with good tolerance. Patient coughed productively during and after session. SpO2 maintained 93-94% throughout.

ventilatorRiskAssessment.currentRisk: Low — patient maintaining adequate oxygenation on low-flow O2, no signs of impending respiratory failure
ventilatorRiskAssessment.riskFactors: Significant comorbidities (HFrEF, obesity), moderate severity pneumonia
ventilatorRiskAssessment.plan: Continue to monitor. If clinical deterioration, early escalation preferred — high-flow nasal cannula → non-invasive ventilation (BiPAP) before intubation if possible.

plan: Continue O2 via NC 2L/min, titrate to SpO2 92-96%
Incentive spirometry q1h while awake, goal 1,000 mL by discharge
Chest physiotherapy BID with modified positioning for HF
Albuterol nebulizer PRN for wheezing (not currently needed)
Will reassess daily — wean O2 as tolerated, advance IS goals
Monitor for clinical deterioration requiring escalation of respiratory support

attestation:
Respiratory therapy assessment completed. O2 therapy initiated and incentive spirometry instructed with return demonstration. Plan of care established.

Electronically signed by: James Parker, RRT
Date/Time: 2/10/2021 11:30 AM
